checks.a_analysis: I reviewed the output JSON against the source PDF pages. All text provided in the JSON, including headers like 'Webpage Analytics: Site Summary' and specific bullet points in the agenda and minutes, correspond directly to the content of the source document. I found no evidence of invented content or fabricated sections.

I verified all URLs and referral sites listed in the JSON (e.g., in Section 11 and Section 9) against the source document (Pages 13 and 11 respectively). All URLs such as 'https://durhamnc.gov/3774/City-Jobs-Careers' and referrers like 'm.facebook.com/' are accurately transcribed from the source. No phone numbers or emails were present to verify, but no new ones were added.

Comparing the page content of the source PDF with the output JSON reveals that several pages are missing. Specifically, content from Page 5 (Title slide), Page 6 (Campaign Overview), Page 7 (Spectrum Reach Campaign metrics), Page 14 (URLs Accessed summary), and Page 15 (Spectrum Reach and Website Analytics) is entirely absent. The reading order in the JSON shows significant gaps where these pages should be (e.g., jumping from reading order index 29 to 47, and 113 to 122) without any placeholder indicating they were intentionally excluded for the snippet.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source document contains Page 6 titled 'Campaign Overview' with bullet points regarding the partnership with Spectrum Reach, and Page 14 titled 'URLs Accessed from the Recruitment Webpage' with statistics on clicks.
checks.b_evidence_output: The output JSON lacks any mention of the 'Campaign Overview' or 'URLs Accessed' content. The reading order jumps from index 29 (end of Page 4) to index 47 (start of Page 8), and from index 113 (end of Page 13) to index 122 (start of Page 16).

checks.a_analysis: I counted the rows in every table in the source and compared them to the JSON output. The Attendees table (Page 2), Vote tables (Page 4), Demographics and Zip code tables (Page 8), Top Cities table (Page 12), Top Clicks table (Page 13), and PSAP Impacts table (Page 16) all have the correct number of data rows in the JSON.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: I reviewed the text content within each section. For sections that were included, such as the Minutes (Pages 2-4) and the PSAP Impacts table (Page 16), the text is preserved accurately without significant omissions. Key information, such as the 'Additional Information' column in the PSAP table, is fully present.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the output matches the source nearly character-for-character, allowing for minor OCR variations and intended reformatting (e.g., adding asterisks for bold/italic in agenda items). Numerical data in tables (Pages 8, 12, 13, 16) matches exactly. No meaning-altering changes were found in the transcribed sections.

checks.a_analysis: The output was reviewed for duplicated text blocks. Intentional repetitions present in the source, such as document headers ('North Carolina 911 Board') and dashboard titles appearing on multiple slides, are correctly preserved. No erroneous duplication of paragraphs or sections was identified.

The heading hierarchy is consistent and logical. H1 is used for the main document titles, H2 for major sections like 'Agenda' and slide titles, and H3 for sub-sections in the minutes. There are no instances of skipped levels (e.g., H1 to H3). Promotion of bolded lines to headings for navigation purposes is noted as an acceptable change for accessibility.

Major tables such as the Agenda (page 1), Attendees (page 2), and PSAP Impacts (page 16) are correctly structured with the appropriate number of columns and header rows. The complex multi-column zip code table on page 8 (Reading Order 49) was parsed accurately into a consistent row/column format that matches the visual layout of the source.
The reading order follows the logical flow of the document, moving through the agenda, the minutes, and finally the presentation slides in the same order they appear in the source PDF.

Several ordered (numbered) lists in the source document have been converted to unordered lists in the JSON output. Furthermore, the item numbers (e.g., '1.', '2.') have been stripped from the content of the list items, resulting in a loss of the original structural meaning.
checks.b_evidence_source: Ordered lists on Page 2 (Agenda items 1-3), Page 3 (Minutes items 4-7), Page 4 (item 9), and Page 17 (ROI items 1-4).
checks.b_evidence_output: Reading Order 15, 18, 23, and 125 are all defined as 'list_type': 'unordered' and the numerical prefix is removed from the text content.

checks.a_analysis: The URLs present in the table on page 13 are correctly captured as text strings matching the source. No broken, missing (intended as clickable), or fabricated links were found.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output successfully maintains the visual organization and flow of the source document. The agenda, meeting minutes, and subsequent slide deck are presented in the same linear order. While the slide content has been reflowed into standard HTML sections with headings and tables for accessibility, the relationship between titles and their respective content remains clear and matches the source's intent.

All tables from the source document, including the complex attendee rosters and the data-heavy PSAP Impacts table, have been correctly rendered in HTML. The columns are properly aligned, headers are visually distinct, and the data remains legible and logically structured.

The HTML output is missing several meaningful and non-decorative images that are present in the source PDF slides. Specifically, the geographic map of the Durham Zone (page 8), the collage of network logos (page 9), and multiple Google Analytics data visualization charts (pages 10, 11, and 12) are absent from the converted output. These elements are critical for understanding the data presented in the slides.
checks.b_evidence_source: Geographic map of Durham Zone (p. 8), network logo collage (p. 9), Google Analytics charts including line graph, bar chart, and pie charts (pp. 10, 11, 12).
checks.b_evidence_output: The rendered HTML for these sections contains the text and tables but excludes the corresponding maps, logos, and charts.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections corresponding to PDF pages 8, 9, 10, 11, and 12.

checks.a_analysis: There are no instances of duplicated body content (paragraphs, tables, or sections) in the rendered HTML output. Titles and headers appropriately appear in both the navigation/TOC area and the main body, which is standard accessible practice.
